Farmers in Uganda can benefit from the new nucleus farmer model, connecting them to mechanization, financing, and markets. Contact your local District Agricultural Extension Officer for more information.
Farmers and stakeholders in the potato value chain are encouraged to familiarize themselves with the recently passed potato regulations by the National Potato Council of Kenya (NPCK).
Tea farmers are encouraged to stay informed about fluctuating tea prices and market trends to make better farming decisions. Please monitor local updates for support and resources available.

Agricultural Extension Service Providers in Uganda are encouraged to adopt the new Ethical Code of Conduct to enhance service delivery. Access the code here: http://agriculture.go.ug/…/Ethical-Code-of-Conduct-for-Agri…

The Aquaculture Business Development Programme is set to launch in 15 counties, starting with pilot projects in Nyeri, Meru, Kirinyaga, Kakamega, Homa Bay, and Migori. Engage with local agricultural offices for participation details.

The Kenyan government will construct three dams in West Pokot, Turkana, and Marsabit to address water scarcity. Counties must identify suitable sites for the dams by next week. Completion is expected by June 2020.
Import, export, and transit of live animals and animal products in Uganda requires authorization from the Commissioner for Animal Health. Ensure compliance with disease control guidelines.

The 2019 Africa Food Prize recognizes exceptional farmers and advocates for innovative and sustainable agricultural practices. Learn from winners like Dr. Emma Naluyima and Baba Dioum to enhance your farming methods.

The African Green Revolution Forum (AGRF) is taking place in Accra, Ghana from September 3-6. Farmers and agribusiness stakeholders are encouraged to engage in discussions on digital transformation in agriculture.
Farmers in Muranga County can access a stable market for milk through 45 co-operatives and sell to Muranga County Creameries at Kshs. 35 per liter.
Farmers and agribusiness stakeholders are encouraged to engage in discussions and share knowledge about potato and sweet potato production for sustainable food systems.
Eligible youth in Uganda can access opportunities under the new National Strategy for Youth Employment in Agriculture. Engage with the Ministry of Agriculture for details on participation.

The Agriculture Cluster Development Project (ACDP) has been expanded to 19 new districts in Uganda, enabling registered farmers to access subsidized farm inputs. Farmers must contribute 33% of the input cost.
Farmers can now access agricultural guidance via the new Kilimo Call Centre's toll-free line. Youth groups and ICT experts are encouraged to utilize this initiative for job creation in agriculture.
The Agricultural Sector Transformation and Growth Strategy 2019-2029 is now in effect, guiding farmers and agribusiness stakeholders to align with national agricultural policies.
Farmers in the Netherlands must register all livestock and obtain a Unique Business Number (UBN) for compliance. Ensure proper tagging and on-farm records.
Small-scale tea farmers are encouraged to enhance the quality of their produce to secure better prices.

Bungoma County has established a Sh230 million Agriculture Institutions Development Corporation to modernize and finance key agricultural institutions. Farmers are encouraged to engage with this new body for support in agricultural development.

Kenyan coffee growers can benefit from direct sales, which yielded USD 49.24 million this season — an average of USD 406 per 50-kg bag, nearly 20% more than auction prices. Consider selling directly for better earnings.
SACCO members are assured that their savings are not at risk; claims about government access to these funds for infrastructure projects are false and legally unfounded.
Uasin Gishu entrepreneurs can now engage with proposed policy reforms and financing opportunities. Stay informed on the Draft MSME Policy 2025 and the MSE Amendment Bill 2025.
The Nairobi Coffee Exchange reports that the recent auction sold 412 tonnes of coffee, translating to a gross return of KES 129 per kilogram of cherry.
Murang'a County is offering commercial mango farming support, including contracts with juice manufacturers and cooperative formation. Farmers are encouraged to participate for improved income.
The government has authorized the duty-free importation of 500,000 metric tonnes of rice, impacting local farmers. Urgent action is needed to clear unsold rice stock before imports begin.
Farmers are facing increased costs due to high animal feed prices driven by a concentrated market. Stay informed about market changes and seek alternative suppliers to manage costs effectively.
Tanzanian and Malawian trade in agricultural products will resume on May 2, 2025. Stakeholders should prepare for this reopening of commercial ties.
Tanzania warns of an impending ban on all agricultural imports from Malawi and South Africa if their current trade restrictions are not lifted by Wednesday next week.

The COMESA Court of Justice has ruled on the use of safeguard measures, emphasizing that countries must avoid arbitrary trade barriers. Stakeholders should stay informed on trade regulations to ensure compliance.
Chege Kirundi has been elected as the new Chairman of KTDA Holdings Board. Tea farmers should stay informed of any upcoming changes in policies or operations.
Farmers are encouraged to adopt the new Waithera bean variety, which offers higher yields and resilience to climate change. Contact KALRO for more information on seed availability.

Farmers and agribusiness stakeholders are encouraged to adopt research-driven technologies to improve livelihoods and achieve food security. Stay informed on innovations following the KALRO conference.
Farmers and agribusiness stakeholders are encouraged to adopt climate-smart water and soil management practices to enhance agricultural resilience against climate change impacts.
Farmers in Uganda can now access a locally developed anti-tick vaccine to combat tick-borne livestock diseases. Visit the National Livestock Resources Research Institute for more information.
Farmers can cultivate high-yielding cassava and other GMOs if they choose; however, regulatory oversight and ongoing legal challenges are in place.
Murang'a County has launched a pilot digital system for distributing certified maize seeds and fertilizer. Approximately 135,000 registered farmers will benefit from this initiative.
Kiawamururu Farmers Cooperative is now offering a payout of Ksh 142.30 per kilogram of cherry and Ksh 470.50 for Mbuni for the 2025/2026 season.
Farmers in coffee-growing regions are encouraged to maintain their coffee trees as the 2025/2026 season is marked by strong market performance and robust demand for Kenyan coffee.
The Nairobi Coffee Exchange held Sale 29, trading 14,284 bags of coffee before July recess; trading resumes in July.
Kenyan farmers are encouraged to continue exporting coffee, as over 1 million kilograms were shipped in March 2026, with strong demand and prices from the U.S. and other international markets.
Tomato prices in Kisumu have nearly doubled due to heavy rains disrupting supply chains. Expect prices at Kibuye Market to range between Sh15 and Sh30 per piece.
Farmers in Nyakach are encouraged to participate in the Agoro East Aggregated Farm initiative to restore land and improve livelihoods. Join the collective effort for better farming practices.
Flower exporters are urged to implement enhanced pest control measures against the false codling moth (FCM) to avoid shipment rejections and financial losses.
The Kenya Agricultural Livestock Research Organization (KALRO) is launching a program to develop new pest management technologies and practices to help farmers reduce crop losses.
Farmers affected by maize lethal necrosis disease (MLND) in Bomet County are encouraged to seek information on crop rotation and planting in new areas to prevent further losses.
Farmers are urged to advocate for the timely adoption of biotech maize, cotton, and potato varieties to avoid losses estimated at KSh 20.4 billion and improve food security.

The CGIAR Africa Agricultural Adaptation (AAA) Atlas is now available as an open-access tool for policymakers and researchers to develop climate-resilient strategies. Utilize this resource to strengthen decision-making in your agricultural projects.

Farmers and agribusiness stakeholders are reminded to ensure compliance with safety and quality standards for pest control products. Stay informed on regulations from the Pest Control Products Board (PCPB).

National Plant Protection Organizations in 12 African countries are being equipped with tools and training to combat Potato Cyst Nematodes. Early detection is crucial for protecting potato crops and livelihoods.
The new Waithera bean variety is now available for farmers, offering higher yields, drought tolerance, and improved taste. Contact KALRO for seed distribution details.
Farmers in Nakuru County are invited to participate in the aak-GROW's Stewardship Mashinani initiative to improve safe pesticide usage. Engage with local community efforts for better practices.
Farmers are encouraged to adopt ratooning technology in rice production to cut costs and increase yields by up to 80%. For more information, contact KALRO.

The Kenyan government, in partnership with UNIDO and FAO, is creating pathways to transform the coffee value chain. Stakeholders are encouraged to collaborate and share knowledge for project development.
KALRO invites stakeholders to engage in socio-economic and policy research to enhance the impact of agricultural innovations. For collaboration opportunities, contact KALRO directly.

Kenyan coffee growers are encouraged to explore innovative coffee processing methods, including natural, carbonic maceration, anaerobic fermentation, and honey processing, to meet evolving market demands.
The National Biosafety Authority will host its 13th Annual Biosafety Conference in two and a half weeks to discuss GMOs and biotechnology safety. All stakeholders are encouraged to participate.
Farmers in Kenya's Arid and Semi-Arid Lands are encouraged to adopt cassava cultivation as a resilient solution to food insecurity and climate change challenges.

Farmers and livestock producers can engage with the $27.4 million global initiative aimed at reducing livestock methane emissions through improved genetics and nutrition. For more information, please visit ILRI's website.
